Makes ShipLog actually show changelogs under real-world rate limits, adds a read-only "Update now", and matches Unraid's native button styling.

## Added
- **"Update now" button** in the changelog bubble when an update is available. ShipLog stays **read-only** — the button triggers Unraid's own apply-update for that container; it never writes to the Docker socket itself.
- **Ollama Autodetect via the Docker socket.** It now finds an Ollama running as a container by asking the read-only Docker socket and probing the container's real address (works on `br0`/custom bridges), not just host ports.

## Changed
- **Changelog shows the pending update's own release notes** (the target release) instead of assembling the whole version span — far fewer registry/GitHub calls, so it shows something more often. The optional Ollama summary still runs over those notes.
- **Buttons match Unraid's native style + hover** via the theme variables (`--button`/`--button-hover`), in both the bubble and the settings page (Apply). The bubble link is now labelled **Repository** (a project may not live on GitHub).
- The **status page** shows the real ShipLog logo instead of the anchor glyph.

## Fixed
- **HTTP 429 robustness** for registries like ghcr.io / lscr.io (LinuxServer): honours `Retry-After` with bounded backoff, throttles requests per registry host, reuses your `GITHUB_TOKEN` to authenticate those registries, lowers sweep concurrency, and keeps the last-known result instead of showing "unknown" on a transient 429. **Set a GitHub token** (Settings → check button shows your limit) to mostly avoid 429s and empty changelogs.

## Install / update
Update from the Plugins tab, or install by URL:
`https://raw.githubusercontent.com/junkerderprovinz/shiplog/main/plugin/shiplog.plg`
